title: Ongoing Brain Activity and Its Role in Cognition: Dual versus Baseline Models.

assets.abstract: What is the role of the brain's ongoing activity for cognition? The predominant perspectives associate ongoing brain activity with resting state, the default-mode network (DMN), and internally oriented mentation. This triad is often contrasted with task states, non-DMN brain networks, and externally oriented mentation, together comprising a "dual model" of brain and cognition. In opposition to this duality, however, we propose that ongoing brain activity serves as a neuronal baseline; this build

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Non-fabrication — spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, creative insight, DMN.
